Introduction

17 January 2016

I want to keep this introduction brief.This purpose of these posts are to track my progress in becoming a developer and will try to not stray off into bigger topics.

I’m not an absolute beginner. I have been coding for about a year but much more seriously in the last 6 months. Up until this point I have gone through quite a few tutorials, mostly related to ruby, rails, html, bootstrap, and some javascript.

The following year will be my transition from an English teacher in South Korea to web developer back in the US.

What to do in 2016:

  • Strengthen my ruby coding skills
  • Develop best practices for developing apps
  • Make TDD second nature
  • Strengthen Javascript
  • Work with Api's and data
  • Build some small (hopefully interesting) projects in rails

I won’t atempt to try and go though all the things I’ve worked up until this point. Lets just say at some point daily coding started to feel like an addiction and I have been spending 15 to 20 hours a week trying to strengthen my skills. Mostly books, tutorials, copying apps, paid memberships, online courses… typical beginner stuff.

For 2016 I have rearanged my work schedule to accomidate for more time to learn development. If you have any desire to discuss the topics in the following post never hesitate to send a message. nateplipp@gmail.com

Github | Twitter
